description: Build the professional React frontend with Vite - including landing page, authentication pages, dashboard with WhatsApp management, and tier/pricing display. Modern UI with Tailwind CSS and Lucide icons.
steps: Create client/package.json with dependencies: react, react-dom, react-router-dom, axios, lucide-react, tailwindcss, postcss, autoprefixer, @headlessui/react. DevDeps: vite, @vitejs/plugin-react, typescript, @types/react, @types/react-dom, vitest, @testing-library/react.
Create client/vite.config.ts - Configure React plugin, proxy /api to http://localhost:3001 for dev.
Create client/tsconfig.json - React JSX, strict mode, paths alias @ -> src/.
Create client/tailwind.config.js and client/postcss.config.js for Tailwind CSS setup.
Create client/index.html - Root HTML with meta tags, favicon placeholder, app div.
Create client/src/main.tsx - React root render with BrowserRouter.
Create client/src/index.css - Tailwind directives (@tailwind base, components, utilities) plus custom professional styles: gradient backgrounds, glass morphism cards, smooth transitions.
Create client/src/App.tsx - Route definitions: / (Landing), /login, /register, /dashboard, /dashboard/sessions, /dashboard/api-keys, /dashboard/pricing. Protected route wrapper for dashboard routes.
Create client/src/contexts/AuthContext.tsx - React context for authentication state. Handles login, register, logout, stores JWT in localStorage, provides user object.
Create client/src/lib/api.ts - Axios instance with base URL, auth interceptor that adds JWT token, response interceptor for 401 handling.

Create client/src/pages/Landing.tsx - Professional landing page with: hero section (gradient background, catchy headline about WhatsApp API gateway, CTA buttons), features grid (icons from lucide-react: MessageSquare, Shield, Zap, Users), pricing section showing 3 tiers with feature comparison, footer with links.
Create client/src/pages/Login.tsx - Clean login form with email/password fields, link to register, error handling, redirect to dashboard on success.
Create client/src/pages/Register.tsx - Registration form with name, email, password, confirm password. Validation, error display, redirect to dashboard on success.
Create client/src/pages/Dashboard.tsx - Dashboard layout with sidebar navigation (Sessions, API Keys, Usage, Pricing). Shows overview: current tier, active sessions count, daily requests used/limit.

Create client/src/pages/Sessions.tsx - WhatsApp sessions management page. Shows list of connected numbers with status indicators (green=connected, yellow=connecting, red=disconnected). 'Connect New Number' button opens modal with phone number input, then displays pairing code in large font for user to enter in WhatsApp.
Create client/src/pages/ApiKeys.tsx - API key management: generate new keys, display existing keys (masked), copy to clipboard, revoke keys.
Create client/src/pages/Pricing.tsx - Detailed pricing page with tier comparison table. Free/Pro/Enterprise cards with features list, pricing, and upgrade buttons.
Create client/src/components/Layout.tsx - Shared layout with navigation bar (logo, nav links, user menu dropdown with logout).

Create client/src/components/PairingCodeModal.tsx - Modal component that shows during WhatsApp pairing: displays the 8-digit code prominently, instructions for user, auto-closes on successful connection (polls status endpoint).
Create client/src/components/SessionCard.tsx - Card component for each WhatsApp session showing phone number, connection status, last active time, disconnect button.
Create client/src/components/PricingCard.tsx - Reusable pricing tier card with: tier name, price, feature list with check/x icons, CTA button. Highlighted style for recommended tier.
acceptance_criteria: Running pnpm --filter client run build produces a dist folder without errors
Landing page renders with hero section, features grid, and pricing section
Login and Register pages connect to backend API endpoints
Dashboard shows user tier info and session count
Sessions page can initiate WhatsApp connection and display pairing code
The UI is responsive and looks professional with Tailwind styling
All routes are properly protected - unauthenticated users redirected to login
findings: Build and tests pass cleanly. The CSS file has slightly different hash on each build due to Tailwind purging. tsconfig.tsbuildinfo is generated by tsc -b and should be committed. React Router shows v7 deprecation warnings in tests but these are informational only.
